days.title: The Tyranny of the Rocket Equation
days.essence: Sum a fuel formula (mass/3 - 2) over every module to learn the I/O format. Part 2 makes fuel itself require fuel, a tidy recursive/iterative accumulation until it bottoms out at zero.

days.title: 1202 Program Alarm
days.essence: First build of the Intcode interpreter (add/multiply/halt with position addressing). Part 2 brute-force searches the 100x100 noun/verb space to hit a target output.

days.title: Crossed Wires
days.essence: Two wires traced as axis-aligned segments; find their crossings, scoring first by Manhattan distance then by combined step count. The clean solution is segment-intersection geometry rather than rasterizing every cell.

days.title: Secure Container
days.essence: Count integers in a range whose digits are non-decreasing and contain an adjacent repeat. Part 2 tightens the repeat rule to an exactly-two group; small enough to count by brute force over the range.

days.title: Sunny with a Chance of Asteroids
days.essence: Extend Intcode with I/O, immediate parameter modes, and the jump/less-than/equals opcodes. The difficulty is careful, bug-free implementation of the growing instruction set, not any single insight.

days.title: Universal Orbit Map
days.essence: Build the orbit tree and sum every object's depth for part 1. Part 2 is a shortest hop count between YOU and SAN, a plain BFS on the orbit graph.

days.title: Amplification Circuit
days.essence: Chain five Intcode amplifiers and try every phase-setting permutation to maximize the signal. Part 2 wires them into a feedback loop, forcing the VM to pause/resume on output so the amplifiers run as cooperating coroutines.

days.title: Space Image Format
days.essence: Slice a flat digit stream into stacked WxH layers; part 1 is a per-layer digit count, part 2 flattens the layers by first non-transparent pixel to render readable text.

days.title: Sensor Boost
days.essence: Finish the Intcode VM with the relative-base mode and unbounded (default-zero, big-integer) memory. With this the interpreter is feature-complete and reused by most later days.

days.title: Monitoring Station
days.essence: Pick the asteroid seeing the most others, grouping the rest by exact line-of-sight angle. Part 2 fires a clockwise-rotating laser, vaporizing the nearest asteroid per angle in sweep order to find the 200th destroyed.

days.title: Space Police
days.essence: An Intcode brain drives a painting robot over an unbounded grid, reading the current tile colour and emitting paint+turn commands. Part 2 starts on white and renders the painted hull as a readable registration identifier.

days.title: The N-Body Problem
days.essence: Simulate four moons under pairwise gravity for 1000 steps in part 1. Part 2's universe-sized period is intractable to brute force; the trick is that each axis cycles independently, so you detect per-axis periods and combine them with an LCM.

days.title: Care Package
days.essence: An Intcode program is a Breakout arcade cabinet emitting (x, y, tile) triples. Part 2 inserts a quarter and you must feed joystick input each frame, steering the paddle to track the ball until every block is cleared.

days.title: Space Stoichiometry
days.essence: Reactions form a DAG with batch quantities and leftover surplus; resolve how much ORE one FUEL needs by recursing through the dependency graph. Part 2 inverts it, binary-searching the maximum FUEL producible from a trillion ORE.

days.title: Oxygen System
days.essence: An Intcode repair droid only reveals the maze as it bumps into walls, so you must explore blindly (DFS/BFS) to map it and find the shortest route to the oxygen system. Part 2 BFS-floods oxygen from that point to count the minutes to fill the area.

days.title: Flawed Frequency Transmission
days.essence: Repeatedly transform a digit list with a positional 0,1,0,-1 pattern for 100 phases. Part 2's input is 10000x longer with a huge offset; the insight is that past the halfway point the pattern is all 1s, so each digit is a simple suffix sum computed back-to-front.

days.title: Set and Forget
days.essence: Intcode renders a scaffold as ASCII; part 1 sums the alignment parameters of its intersections. Part 2's real puzzle is compressing the full traversal path into three reusable movement routines (A/B/C) that each fit the 20-character limit.

days.title: Many-Worlds Interpretation
days.essence: Collect every key in the shortest walk while locked doors gate progress; the state is (position, keys-held bitmask) searched with Dijkstra/BFS over a reduced key-to-key graph. Part 2 splits the vault into four robots that must be coordinated jointly, which the solver left unfinished.

days.title: Tractor Beam
days.essence: Probe the beam point by point with Intcode and count hits in a 50x50 block. Part 2 exploits the beam's straight diverging edges, walking down them to locate the first place a 100x100 ship square fits entirely inside.

days.title: Donut Maze
days.essence: A donut-shaped maze whose lettered portals teleport between matched tiles; part 1 is a BFS shortest path treating each portal as an edge. Part 2 turns inner/outer portals into recursive maze levels, making it a search through a layered grid (left unsolved).

days.title: Springdroid Adventure
days.essence: Hand-author a tiny boolean springscript program (AND/OR/NOT over ground sensors into T and J registers) so the droid jumps exactly when it must. Part 2 extends sensing to nine tiles, requiring a more careful logic formula within the 15-instruction budget.

days.title: Slam Shuffle
days.essence: Three shuffle moves rearrange a deck; part 1 just tracks one card's final index. Part 2 is notorious: a 119-billion-card deck shuffled 101-billion times, solved only by composing the shuffle as a linear (ax+b mod n) map and applying modular exponentiation plus modular inverse.

days.title: Category Six
days.essence: Run 50 networked Intcode computers that route (X, Y) packets by address, with empty input queues returning -1. Part 1 watches for the first packet to address 255; part 2 adds a NAT that revives an idle network and asks for the first Y it delivers twice in a row.

days.title: Planet of Discord
days.essence: Evolve a 5x5 Game-of-Life grid until a layout repeats, scoring its biodiversity rating. Part 2 makes the centre tile a recursively nested grid, so cells gain neighbours on adjacent levels and you simulate an expanding stack of folded grids.

days.title: Cryostasis
days.essence: An Intcode text adventure where you walk rooms and pick up items, here driven through an interactive ASCII REPL. The lock is the pressure-sensitive checkpoint: search over subsets of carried items to match the required weight and pass.
